skill.name: Apple HIG: Interaction Patterns
skill.description: A comprehensive reference skill for Apple Human Interface Guidelines interaction patterns. Provides expert guidance on iOS and macOS UX patterns including onboarding, loading states, drag and drop, search, settings, notifications, modality, multitasking, feedback, haptics, audio, video, and more. Use when building Apple platform apps.
skill.summary: Apple HIG interaction patterns reference for iOS and macOS apps
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. This is a documentation/reference skill containing Apple HIG pattern descriptions. The scanner incorrectly flagged common documentation terms (URLs, Keychain, crypto, shell commands in code blocks) as security issues. No executable code, no network calls, no external commands - pure reference documentation.

content.value_statement: Building Apple platform apps requires following Human Interface Guidelines for a consistent user experience. This skill provides expert guidance on 25+ interaction patterns including onboarding, loading, navigation, and platform-specific implementations.
actual_capabilities: Provides step-by-step guidance for 25+ Apple HIG interaction patterns
Recommends appropriate UI patterns based on user goals and context
Explains platform-specific variations for iOS, iPadOS, and macOS
Identifies common pitfalls that violate Apple HIG
Covers accessibility considerations for each pattern
References official Apple documentation and best practices
limitations: Does not provide code implementations - only design guidance
Does not cover Android or Web platform patterns
Does not include visual assets or design files
Requires understanding of SwiftUI or UIKit fundamentals

use_cases.title: Design onboarding flow
use_cases.description: Create a brief, effective onboarding experience following Apple HIG principles with progressive disclosure and skip options

use_cases.title: Implement loading states
use_cases.description: Choose appropriate loading patterns including activity indicators, progress bars, and skeleton screens based on wait time and context

use_cases.title: Handle destructive actions
use_cases.description: Design safe destructive actions with undo support instead of excessive confirmation dialogs per Apple HIG recommendations

prompt_templates.title: Onboarding guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: How should I design onboarding for my iOS app? What are the Apple HIG recommendations for first-time user experience?

prompt_templates.title: Loading pattern selection
prompt_templates.prompt: My app takes 5+ seconds to load data. What loading pattern should I use according to Apple HIG?

prompt_templates.title: Modal usage
prompt_templates.prompt: When should I use a modal sheet vs a full-screen modal vs an alert? What does Apple HIG recommend?

prompt_templates.title: Feedback implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: How should I provide feedback to users after they complete an action? What haptics and visual indicators does Apple recommend?

output_examples.input: How should I design onboarding for my iOS app?
output_examples.output: Apple HIG recommends keeping onboarding to 3 screens maximum. Use progressive disclosure to show essentials first. Allow users to skip and teach through contextual hints rather than tutorials. Avoid requiring sign-up upfront - let users explore first.

Use modality only when critical attention is needed, a task must be completed or abandoned, or saving changes is essential. Prefer non-modal alternatives like sheets for optional input. Full-screen modals are for focused tasks.

output_examples.input: How do I handle destructive actions?
output_examples.output: Support undo over confirmation dialogs when possible. Use destructive action buttons with clear labeling (red color). Reserve "Are you sure?" dialogs for irreversible actions with serious consequences.

best_practices: Minimize modality - use sheets and non-blocking UI over full-screen modals whenever possible
Provide immediate feedback - every action should have visible, audible, or haptic response
Support undo over confirmation dialogs - make destructive actions reversible when feasible
anti_patterns: Splash screens with logos - show meaningful content immediately with launch screen transitioning seamlessly
Requesting all permissions at launch - request permissions contextually when needed
Excessive "Are you sure?" dialogs - prefer undo support and clear destructive button styling over confirmation fatigue

faq.question: What is the maximum number of onboarding screens recommended?
faq.answer: Apple HIG recommends a maximum of 3 onboarding screens. Keep it brief, allow users to skip, and teach through progressive disclosure.

faq.question: When should I use a modal vs a sheet?
faq.answer: Use sheets for optional tasks that can be dismissed. Use full-screen modals for focused tasks requiring completion. Use alerts for critical attention only.

faq.question: How do I handle long loading times?
faq.answer: Use skeleton screens for unknown wait times, progress bars for known durations, and activity indicators for brief waits. Never show a blank screen.

faq.question: Should I ask for sign-in immediately?
faq.answer: No, defer sign-in. Let users explore your app first. Use Sign in with Apple and support passkeys for authentication.

faq.question: How should I implement search?
faq.answer: Use search bar with suggestions, support scoped search, show recent searches, and provide results with highlighting.

faq.question: What is progressive disclosure?
faq.answer: Show essential information and controls first, then reveal advanced options as users need them. Avoid overwhelming users with every option upfront.
